market.question: Will Switzerland win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
market.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

assumptionAudits.marketQuestion: Will Switzerland win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?

assumptions.assumption: Switzerland must be drawn into a favorable group and knockout bracket path that avoids top-tier teams (e.g., France, Brazil, Argentina, England) until at least the semifinals, requiring a relatively easy draw at the World Cup group stage allocation on a specific date.
assumptions.breakingEvent: 2026 FIFA World Cup group stage draw (expected late 2025, exact date TBD by FIFA) placing Switzerland in a group with two or more strong opponents and/or a bracket path requiring them to face a top-5 ranked team before the quarterfinals.
assumptions.timeToBreak: Late 2025 (exact date TBD, typically November/December of the year before the tournament), approximately 7-8 months before resolution

assumptionAudits.marketQuestion: Will Switzerland win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?

assumptions.assumption: Switzerland must be drawn into a favorable group and knockout bracket path that avoids top-tier teams (e.g., France, Brazil, Argentina, England) until at least the semifinals, requiring a relatively easy draw at the 2025 FIFA World Cup group stage draw.
assumptions.breakingEvent: 2026 FIFA World Cup group stage draw (expected late 2025) places Switzerland in a difficult group (e.g., with Brazil, France, or Argentina) or a knockout bracket path requiring them to beat multiple elite teams consecutively.
assumptions.timeToBreak: Late 2025 (exact date TBD by FIFA, typically November-December 2025), approximately 7-8 months before resolution

assumptions.assumption: Switzerland's "Golden Generation" of players (e.g., Akanji, Xhaka, Shaqiri, Embolo, Sommer) will remain at or near peak form and availability through 2026, and no structural decline in Swiss player development or national team cohesion will occur before the tournament.
assumptions.breakingEvent: Key Swiss players announce international retirement before 2026, or a major injury sidelines a core player (e.g., Manuel Akanji or Granit Xhaka) for the tournament, or Switzerland fails to qualify for the knockout stage of UEFA Euro 2024/2028, revealing structural decline.
assumptions.timeToBreak: Between now and June 2026 — with key inflection points at Euro 2024 (June-July 2024), Nations League cycles, and qualification draws

assumptionAudits.marketQuestion: Will Switzerland win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?

assumptions.assumption: Switzerland must be drawn into a favorable group and knockout bracket path that avoids top-tier teams (e.g., France, Brazil, Argentina, England) until at least the semifinals, requiring a relatively easy draw at the World Cup Final Draw.
assumptions.breakingEvent: 2026 FIFA World Cup Final Draw results (expected late 2025), placing Switzerland in a group with multiple elite teams or a knockout bracket path requiring them to beat multiple top-5 ranked nations consecutively.
assumptions.timeToBreak: Late 2025 (approximately December 2025), roughly 7 months before the tournament begins

assumptionAudits.marketQuestion: Will Switzerland win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?

assumptions.assumption: Switzerland must be drawn into a favorable group and knockout bracket path that avoids top-tier teams (e.g., France, Brazil, Argentina, England) until at least the semifinals, requiring a relatively weak Group A or B placement and a manageable Round of 16 opponent.
assumptions.breakingEvent: 2026 FIFA World Cup group stage draw (expected late 2025, exact date TBD by FIFA)
assumptions.timeToBreak: Late 2025 (approximately 7-8 months before resolution), when the draw reveals Switzerland's group and potential knockout path

assumptions.assumption: Switzerland has a realistic path to winning the 2026 FIFA World Cup despite never having won it before and being a mid-tier European team.
assumptions.breakingEvent: Switzerland is eliminated in the group stage or Round of 16 of the 2026 FIFA World Cup
assumptions.timeToBreak: June-July 2026 (group stage and knockout rounds), earliest possible elimination ~June 15-18, 2026 (Matchday 3 of group stage)

assumptionAudits.marketQuestion: Will Switzerland win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?

assumptions.assumption: Switzerland must be drawn into a favorable group and knockout bracket path that avoids top-tier teams (e.g., France, Brazil, Argentina, England) until at least the semifinals, given their Elo ranking (~15th-20th globally) and historical performance (never past quarterfinals since 1954).
assumptions.breakingEvent: 2026 FIFA World Cup group stage draw (expected late 2025, exact date TBD by FIFA, likely November/December 2025)
assumptions.timeToBreak: Approximately 7-8 months before resolution (draw expected late 2025)

opinions.name: Fundamentals Analyst
opinions.reasoning: [high] Switzerland must be drawn into a favorable group and knockout bracket path that avoids top-tier teams (e.g., France, Brazil, Argentina, England) until at least the semifinals, requiring a relatively easy draw at the World Cup group stage allocation on a specific date. | 2026 FIFA World Cup group stage draw (expected late 2025, exact date TBD by FIFA) placing Switzerland in a group with two or more strong opponents and/or a bracket path requiring them to face a top-5 ranked team before the quarterfinals.

opinions.name: Event Horizon
opinions.reasoning: [high] Switzerland has a realistic path to winning the 2026 FIFA World Cup, requiring them to advance through group stage and multiple knockout rounds. | Switzerland is eliminated in the group stage or first knockout round of the 2026 FIFA World Cup

opinions.name: Contrarian
opinions.reasoning: [high] Switzerland's "golden generation" of players (e.g., Akanji, Xhaka, Shaqiri, Embolo, Sommer) will still be at or near their peak performance levels in 2026, and the team's core structural advantages (defensive organization, tournament experience, group-stage consistency) will translate into a knockout-stage run deep enough to win the tournament. | Key Swiss players (e.g., Granit Xhaka, Manuel Akanji, Yann Sommer) suffer significant decline, retire from international duty, or sustain long-term injuries before or during the 2026 World Cup; or Switzerland fails to qualify from their group in 2026.
